prompt:
A spring-loaded wire stripper lies on a work surface, its jaws partly open to reveal multiple notched holes labeled for different wire gauges, with a short piece of insulated wire positioned ready to be stripped. The built-in cutter section near the pivot shows a cleanly snipped wire end, and the handles are covered in simple non-slip grips with no visible branding. A few small lengths of insulation and bare copper wires scattered nearby make it clear that the tool has been actively used for stripping 24–10 AWG conductors.

The environment is a near-future solarpunk electronics and wiring bench with soft, even lighting, neatly bundled cables and a modular power distribution panel in the blurred background, plus a small plant adding a touch of green. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or readable markings, and a composition that centers the wire stripper, partially stripped wire, and small offcuts while the rest of the workspace remains softly out of focus.
